metadata.summary: Create AI-readable AGENTS.md (20-80 lines) for every code directory, paired with CLAUDE.md containing @AGENTS.md. Enables agents to navigate codebases without hallucinating file paths or structure.

content.title: Documentation (AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md)
content.summary: Create AI-readable documentation pairs for every code directory: CLAUDE.md (one line: @AGENTS.md) and AGENTS.md (20-80 lines describing what the dir is, its files, entry points, and gotchas). Without this pair, agents load no context and scan files blindly, wasting tokens and hallucinating structure.

why.p: AI agents entering a codebase cold need a machine-readable routing doc to decide which files to load. Without AGENTS.md, the agent must scan all files to form context, wasting tokens and hallucinating structure. Two-pass generation (discovery → write → verify) keeps docs accurate; a nightly drift check catches stale tables before they mislead agents.

list.item: Bootstrapping AI-readable docs in a new repo, sub-package, or refactored directory.
After a refactor where the file tree changed and existing AGENTS.md tables are stale.
Multi-repo monorepos: each package needs its own AGENTS.md per the project convention.
Legacy codebases with only a top-level README — rolling out per-module coverage.
Repos under ~200 lines with one obvious entry point — a README suffices.
Generated/derived directories (dist/, build/, node_modules/) — docs just repeat tooling output.
Short-lived experiments where the doc rots faster than the code iterates.
Public-facing user docs (Docusaurus/Mintlify) — this methodology targets AI readers, not customers.

rule.statement: Every directory containing source code must have both CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md.
rule.rationale: CLAUDE.md is the Claude Code entrypoint (contains only @AGENTS.md). AGENTS.md is auto-loaded and provides routing context. Without the pair, agents load no context and scan files blindly.

rule.statement: CLAUDE.md must contain exactly one line: @AGENTS.md
rule.rationale: Claude Code's @-include mechanism loads AGENTS.md automatically. Any other content in CLAUDE.md is redundant and wastes tokens.

rule.statement: AGENTS.md must be 20-80 lines, hard cap 120. If it cannot answer routing questions in 80 lines, split the directory.
rule.rationale: Auto-loaded context must fit in a small token budget. Long AGENTS.md files delay every agent session and bury key routing signals.

rule.statement: AGENTS.md must answer: what this dir IS, key files with one-line purpose, entry points, and important gotchas.
rule.rationale: An agent entering a module cold needs to decide in one read whether to proceed or route elsewhere. All four questions must be answerable without opening any other file.

rule.statement: Use a file table (Markdown table with Path and Purpose columns) for the structure section. No ASCII art, no box-drawing characters.
rule.rationale: Tables render on GitHub and in terminal. ASCII art breaks on non-monospace fonts and wastes tokens.

rule.statement: Add CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md to every directory that contains source code — not just the repo root, but also subpackages, feature modules, and test dirs.
rule.rationale: Agents navigate to the nearest AGENTS.md. Without per-module coverage, agents at deeper paths get no context and must walk up the tree or scan blindly.

rule.statement: Skip AGENTS.md only in empty __init__.py-only dirs with no logic.
rule.rationale: A file that adds no routing signal wastes maintenance effort and is not loaded in practice.

rule.statement: Every file path mentioned in a file table must correspond to an actual file, verified by Read before committing the doc.
rule.rationale: LLMs hallucinate file paths confidently. An AGENTS.md with broken table entries actively misleads agents, causing worse behavior than no doc at all.

rule.statement: Generate AGENTS.md in two passes: Pass 1 discovers actual files via git ls-files; Pass 2 writes from the outline only — no invention.
rule.rationale: Single-pass generation produces hallucinated file names and invented exports. Separating discovery from writing forces the writer to use only verified inputs.

rule.statement: Pass 3 (verification): re-read the produced doc, open every file/path mentioned, report broken references before committing.
rule.rationale: Broken references in AGENTS.md mislead subsequent agents. Automated verification is cheaper than manual review of every generated doc.

rule.statement: The methodology already ships a universal CLAUDE.md template plus type-specific sections (Backend / Frontend / Infra / Library). Useful agent companion: a discovery script that builds the JSON outline from git ls-files and tokei.
rule.rationale: Pre-built templates reduce hallucination surface; per-type sections handle the most common directory shapes in production codebases.

rule.statement: Cap AGENTS.md at 80 lines and CLAUDE.md at @AGENTS.md only — enforce with markdownlint custom rule.
rule.rationale: Auto-loaded context that exceeds ~80 lines bloats every agent session. Strict enforcement prevents length creep in collaborative authoring scenarios.

rule.statement: Always pair AGENTS.md with a verification step that opens every referenced file; LLMs invent paths confidently.
rule.rationale: Broken references in AGENTS.md actively mislead agents, causing worse behavior than no doc at all.

rule.statement: Co-locate doc generation with code edits in the same PR; nightly drift jobs are a fallback, not the primary path.
rule.rationale: Docs committed in the same PR as code changes are accurate at merge. Docs updated only nightly drift within hours of a refactor.

rule.statement: Use tree-sitter or language-native AST for the API extraction step — regex misses nested classes, decorators, JSX exports.
rule.rationale: Accurate API surface extraction eliminates a major source of hallucinated exports and stale function lists in the doc.

rule.statement: Forbid ASCII art and emojis in the prompt; LLMs default to them.
rule.rationale: ASCII art breaks on non-monospace fonts and wastes tokens; emojis are explicitly forbidden by the project convention.

rule.statement: Add a "Last verified: commit-hash" footer; if the hash is more than N commits behind HEAD, mark stale in CI.
rule.rationale: Makes staleness machine-detectable. Agents can skip reading docs flagged as stale without wasting context on outdated guidance.

rule.statement: For monorepos, generate one AGENTS.md per package and a top-level INDEX.md linking them; the agent reads only what it needs.
rule.rationale: In monorepos with many packages, a single aggregate AGENTS.md balloons; per-package docs keep each focused and let agents navigate efficiently.

example.title: Generator Prompt
example.code: Create AGENTS.md for <dir>. Strict rules: - Length 20-80 lines. - Every entry in the file table must correspond to an actual file (verified by Read). - No ASCII art. Tables, lists, arrows (->), directory trees only. - English only. - If a section has nothing real to say, omit it. Input outline (JSON): <outline> Output: contents of AGENTS.md only, no commentary.
example.note: Pass JSON outline as the only source of file truth. The writer prompt must not be allowed to add files it "knows about" from training data.

example.title: Verifier Prompt
example.code: Verify <path>/AGENTS.md against the actual directory. For every file/symbol mentioned, Read or grep to confirm existence. Output JSON: { "missing_refs": [...], "stale_tables": [...] }.
example.note: Verification prompt. Run after every write; block commit if missing_refs is non-empty.

rule.statement: Run a doc-drift check on every PR that touches a module's source files. A nightly job is a fallback, not the primary path.
rule.rationale: Docs committed in the same PR as code changes are accurate at merge. Docs updated only nightly drift within hours of a refactor.

rule.statement: Add a "Last verified: commit-hash" footer to generated docs. Mark stale in CI if the hash is more than N commits behind HEAD.
rule.rationale: Makes staleness machine-detectable. Agents can skip reading docs flagged as stale.

antipattern.description: Hallucinated files: agent lists files that do not exist in the directory.
antipattern.reason: LLMs confidently generate plausible-sounding file names from training data. AGENTS.md with non-existent paths causes downstream agents to issue failing Reads, breaking workflows.

antipattern.description: Length creep: agent expands AGENTS.md to 200+ lines with redundant prose and bullet lists.
antipattern.reason: Auto-loaded context that exceeds ~80 lines bloats every agent session. Enforce a hard 120-line cap; reject and regenerate if exceeded.

antipattern.description: ASCII art and box-drawing characters in AGENTS.md.
antipattern.reason: LLMs trained on README files default to fancy boxes and banners. These break on non-monospace terminals, waste tokens, and are explicitly forbidden by the project convention.

antipattern.description: Stale tables: file/endpoint/model tables not updated after a refactor.
antipattern.reason: Tables generated from AST or git ls-files stay accurate; hand-edited tables diverge within days. Regenerate from source, don't hand-edit after initial creation.

antipattern.description: Self-referential loop: agent reads its own previous draft as source of truth and treats hallucinations as fact.
antipattern.reason: Each generation pass must start from the raw repository state (git ls-files, actual file reads), never from a previous doc version. Previous docs are hypotheses, not ground truth.

antipattern.description: Multi-language confusion: in a monorepo with Python + TS + Go, the agent picks one language and ignores the others.
antipattern.reason: Pass per-package outlines scoped to each language subdirectory, not a whole-repo outline.

antipattern.description: Duplicating parent context: AGENTS.md for a subpackage repeats information already in the parent AGENTS.md.
antipattern.reason: Each AGENTS.md covers only its own directory. Parents are auto-loaded already; duplication wastes tokens and goes stale independently.

rule.statement: First-time generation in a directory should always be human-reviewed before committing. Subsequent updates can be agent-merged if drift checks pass.
rule.rationale: Agents frequently hallucinate on first pass; human review catches these before the bad doc misleads future agents. After initial review, incremental updates are safe to merge automatically.

section.name: cli-and-tooling
section.p: Common tools for doc generation and verification: tree (file listing), eza (modern tree with gitignore), markdownlint-cli2 (Markdown linting), vale (prose linting), lychee (link checking), cspell (spell-checking), tokei (LOC counting), tree-sitter queries or ts-morph/astroid (API extraction), git ls-files (source of truth for what exists).

Services and platforms: GitHub Actions (schedule nightly drift checks), GitHub Copilot Workspace (repo-aware doc updates), Cursor/Zed (inline LLM doc generation), markdownlint GitHub Action (enforce structure rules), Vale with custom style (encode "no ASCII art / no filler" rules).

template.purpose: Universal AGENTS.md skeleton with all required sections.
Discovery script: emits JSON outline from git ls-files and tokei.
